result.stdout:
0.13.0

Usage: csipfs [options] [command]

Options:
  --version    Show version information
  --help       Show help information
  --api <url>  Use a specific API instance
  -L|--local   Run the command locally, instead of using the daemon
  --enc        The output type (json, xml, or text)
  --debug      Show debugging info
  --trace      Show tracing info
  --time       Show how long the command took

Commands:
  add          Add a file to IPFS
  bitswap      Manage swapped blocks
  block        Manage raw blocks
  bootstrap    Manage bootstrap peers
  cat          Show IPFS file data
  config       Manage the configuration
  daemon       Start a long running IPFS deamon
  dht          Query the DHT for values or peers
  dns          Resolve DNS link
  files        Manage the mfs (Mutable File System) [WIP]
  get          Download IPFS data
  id           Show info on an IPFS peer
  init         Initialize ipfs local configuration [WIP]
  key          Manage private keys
  ls           List links
  name         Manage IPNS names
  object       Manage IPFS objects
  pin          Manage data in local storage [WIP]
  pubsub       Publish/subscribe to messages on a given topic
  refs         List hashes of links [WIP]
  repo         Manage the IPFS repository
  resolve      Resolve any type of name
  shutdown     Stop the IPFS deamon
  stats        Query IPFS statistics
  swarm        Manage connections to the p2p network
  update       Download the latest version [WIP]
  version      Show version information

Run 'csipfs [command] --help' for more information about a command.

commands.payload:
0.13.0

Usage: csipfs [options] [command]

Options:
  --version    Show version information
  --help       Show help information
  --api <url>  Use a specific API instance
  -L|--local   Run the command locally, instead of using the daemon
  --enc        The output type (json, xml, or text)
  --debug      Show debugging info
  --trace      Show tracing info
  --time       Show how long the command took

Commands:
  add          Add a file to IPFS
  bitswap      Manage swapped blocks
  block        Manage raw blocks
  bootstrap    Manage bootstrap peers
  cat          Show IPFS file data
  config       Manage the configuration
  daemon       Start a long running IPFS deamon
  dht          Query the DHT for values or peers
  dns          Resolve DNS link
  files        Manage the mfs (Mutable File System) [WIP]
  get          Download IPFS data
  id           Show info on an IPFS peer
  init         Initialize ipfs local configuration [WIP]
  key          Manage private keys
  ls           List links
  name         Manage IPNS names
  object       Manage IPFS objects
  pin          Manage data in local storage [WIP]
  pubsub       Publish/subscribe to messages on a given topic
  refs         List hashes of links [WIP]
  repo         Manage the IPFS repository
  resolve      Resolve any type of name
  shutdown     Stop the IPFS deamon
  stats        Query IPFS statistics
  swarm        Manage connections to the p2p network
  update       Download the latest version [WIP]
  version      Show version information

Run 'csipfs [command] --help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Add a file to IPFS

Usage: csipfs add [arguments] [options]

Arguments:
  path            The path to a file to be added to ipfs

Options:
  --help          Show help information
  --chunk-size    The maximum number of bytes in a block
  --encoding      CID encoding algorithm
  --hash          The hashing algorithm
  -n|--only-hash  Only chunk and hash - do not write to disk
  -p|--progress   
  --pin           Pin when adding
  --raw-leaves    Raw data for leaf nodes
  -t|--trickle    Use trickle dag format
  -w|--wrap       Wrap file in a directory
  -r|--recursive  Add directory paths recursively
  --protect       protect the data with the key

commands.payload:
Add a file to IPFS

Usage: csipfs add [arguments] [options]

Arguments:
  path            The path to a file to be added to ipfs

Options:
  --help          Show help information
  --chunk-size    The maximum number of bytes in a block
  --encoding      CID encoding algorithm
  --hash          The hashing algorithm
  -n|--only-hash  Only chunk and hash - do not write to disk
  -p|--progress   
  --pin           Pin when adding
  --raw-leaves    Raw data for leaf nodes
  -t|--trickle    Use trickle dag format
  -w|--wrap       Wrap file in a directory
  -r|--recursive  Add directory paths recursively
  --protect       protect the data with the key

Manage the configuration

Usage: csipfs config [arguments] [options] [command]

  name     The name of the configuration setting
  value    The value of the configuration setting

  --help   Show help information
  --json   Treat the <value> as JSON

  replace  Replace the config file
  show     Show the config file contents

Run 'config [command] --help' for more information about a command.

Manage private keys

Usage: csipfs key [options] [command]

  --help  Show help information

  export  Export the key to a PKCS #8 PEM file

  rm      Remove the key

Run 'key [command] --help' for more information about a command.

Usage: csipfs pubsub pub [arguments] [options]

Arguments:
  topic    The topic of interest
  message  The data to publish

Options:
  --help   Show help information

result.stdout:
Manage the IPFS repository

Usage: csipfs repo [options] [command]

Options:
  --help   Show help information

Commands:
  gc       Perform a garbage collection sweep on the repo
  migrate  Migrate to the version
  stat     Repository information
  verify   Verify all blocks in repo are not corrupted
  version  Repository version

Run 'repo [command] --help' for more information about a command.

Run 'repo [command] --help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Resolve any type of name

Usage: csipfs resolve [arguments] [options]

Arguments:
  name            The IPFS/IPNS/... name

Options:
  --help          Show help information
  -r|--recursive  Resolve until the result is an IPFS name

result.stdout:
Manage connections to the p2p network

Usage: csipfs swarm [options] [command]

Options:
  --help      Show help information

Commands:
  addrs       List addresses of known peers
  connect     Connect to a peer
  disconnect  Disconnect from a peer
  peers       List of connected peers

Run 'swarm [command] --help' for more information about a command.
